concerns.analysis: The JSON output contains a massive, fabricated string of repeating zeros appended to a Table of Contents entry on page 2. While this is likely an AI extraction artifact caused by parsing leader dots in the table of contents, it is not JSON structure or prompt text (which are the only exceptions allowed). Because this long string of zeros appears nowhere in the source text, it constitutes fabricated content.
concerns.location: Page 2, Table of Contents list item

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The Table of Contents extraction on page 2 terminates abruptly after the fabricated string of zeros. Consequently, roughly 80% of the Table of Contents section (covering sections 3.1.1 through 6.0) is entirely missing from the output JSON.
concerns.output_quote: [Content entirely absent from the list after '3.1 Project Information and Report Organization']

concerns.analysis: Compared clickable hyperlinks in the source PDF against the JSON output. Several text elements that are explicitly hyperlinked with embedded URLs in the source PDF were extracted as plain text blocks, completely missing their hyperlink attributes. This includes important references in the introduction and reference columns in tables.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 5: "Risk Evaluation Resources", "USEPA Regional Screening Level (RSL) Tables", and "Technical Guidance for Risk-Based Environmental Remediation of Sites" are all clickable hyperlinks. Page 24: Table entries in the 'Reference' column (e.g., "IRIS", "Supplemental Guidance") are clickable links.
concerns.output_quote: These are extracted as plain text without any hyperlink or URL data. For example: {"type": "paragraph", "text": "Risk Evaluation Resources"}

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (69): Risk Calculator User Guide; Risk Calculator User Guide; February 2024
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=69, pages_affected=31/33. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "Chemical with a Henry's Law constant less than or ". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "ASTM"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 34 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (31 truncated mid-sentence, 3 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p1: "...y. The design features a blue outline map of North"; p6: "...nmental contaminants from initial release to human"; p8: "... and a black house icon on the right, separated by"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=34 (truncated=31, placeholder=3, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source PDF frequently uses underlines to denote clickable hyperlinks (e.g., 'Risk Evaluation Resources'). Because the JSON format has no markdown representation for underline and did not capture these as markdown links, the text appears as plain text, resulting in a genuine loss of visual emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Risk Evaluation Resources (styled with blue text and underline)
concerns.output_quote: "text": "Risk Evaluation Resources" (styled as plain paragraph text)

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
